basics.name: Ahron Natividad
basics.label: Student
basics.summary: I am studying for a B.S. in Computer Science in the Department of Information and Computer Sciences at the University of Hawaii. I expect to graduate Fall 2027.

education.area: Computer Science, Minor in Mathematics (expected Fall 2027)
courses: ICS 111: Introduction to Computer Science I
ICS 141: Discrete Mathematics for Computer Science
ICS 241: Discrete Mathematics for Computer Science II
ICS 211: Introduction to Computer Science II

education.institution: Institute Français des Alpes, Annecy, France

highlights: Assists faculty/students in daily clerical needs (i.e., photocopying, spreadsheets, answering questions)
Currently in training on learning to operate/utilize Cascade, the University of Hawai’i content management system (CMS) for our IPLL department website
Creation of promotional material for the department programs
Assisted with the drive-thru operations by multitasking between order-taking, cash handling, and rapid preparation of McCafe menu items to ensure wait times were under 90 seconds
Regularly extending 8-hour shifts to assist with closing procedures and coverage during understaffed shifts for the summer months.

volunteer.summary: Teaching 6th - 12th graders on basic web development fundamentals like HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.
highlights: Mentoring 6th-12th graders on basic web development skills like HTML, CSS, and JS every Saturday during the academic spring semesters.
Delivered interactive lectures and coding workshops to spark interest in students for computer science
Helped over 20+ students build their digital literacy skills and passion for web development

volunteer.summary: Assisting the TA as well as helping students with ICS 141/ICS 241 discrete math topics for CS.
highlights: Provided contact hours for those who need additional help after class
Learned about computing ethics in ICS 390
Breaking down difficult concepts for students

awards.title: Academic Merit Scholarship
awards.summary: Merit Scholarship awarded to recognize incoming freshmen students enrolling in UH Manoa with a very strong academic high school record.

awards.title: Alwine & Jesse Doyle Scholarship
awards.awarder: University of Hawaii at Manoa College of Arts, Languages, and Letters
awards.summary: Awarded to students who want to earn credits in one of the languages taught in the Department of Languages & Literatures of Europe & the Americas (French, German, Italian, Portuguese, Russian, and Spanish) at an accredited university in Europe
